card_description: Teilhard de Chardin (French: "Unknown") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the philosophical tradition, Teilhard de Chardin is 20th century Jesuit paleontologist; Omega Point theology. PKD's concept of evolving cosmic consciousness parallels Teilhard's noosphere. Appears in 9 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Teilhard de Chardin. Context: "Dick identifies his 1974 experience as the "Second Advent," occurring pluralistically within individual minds rather than in a single visible man."
